mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates beyond a 0.5 percentage point monthly increase, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity investors to price in lower earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for forward equity returns over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in goods and services demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and forces equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX direction with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between contraction and expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a sequential increase in real GDP growth signals the normalization of corporate cash flows, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors re-rate earnings multiples upward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when GDPC1 shows consecutive quarterly expansions, or the rolling correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The monthly change in INDPRO exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 for 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple market cycles, or forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.25 percent, the effective zero lower bound eliminates conventional monetary policy space, triggering a liquidity-driven risk-on regime that pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent, or the threshold crossing fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.04 over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in nonfarm payrolls and a contraction in retail sales data confirms a demand-driven labor market shock, which accelerates earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint momentum of PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict SPX direction with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between contraction and expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle expansion phase, a sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals improving household income and consumption capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity investors price in higher dividend growth.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE declines for three consecutive months, or the rolling correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.
